skill.name: improve-skill
skill.description: Analyze local Claude Code, Codex, or Pi session transcripts to identify improvements for existing skills or draft new skill instructions.
skill.summary: Extract session transcripts and turn agent usage patterns into focused skill improvement prompts.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static findings for command execution and weak cryptography are mostly false positives caused by shell examples, template literals, and words such as Claude. The confirmed risk is that the bundled script intentionally searches hidden agent directories, reads local session JSONL files, and prints transcript content that may include secrets or private project data.

medium_findings.title: Local Session Transcript Exposure
medium_findings.description: The script searches hidden Claude, Pi, and Codex session directories, reads matching JSONL session files, and prints transcript content. This is expected functionality, but transcripts can contain credentials, private code, project paths, and user instructions, so users need a clear warning before publishing or sharing output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem locations and read operations are explicit, and transcript output is the main purpose of the script. No network exfiltration is present, so the risk is local disclosure through user-directed output.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Local Session File Input
medium_findings.description: The command accepts a user-supplied session file path and reads it if it exists. This is legitimate for a transcript extraction tool, but it can expose any readable local file passed by mistake or by a copied command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is provided by the user and read directly after an existence check. The behavior is not malicious, but it creates a clear local data exposure risk when misused.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Mostly Documentation or Template Literals
low_findings.description: The JavaScript file does not import child_process or execute shell commands at the flagged locations. Most matches are template literals, console output, or Markdown command examples that tell the user how to run the included script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows string interpolation and Markdown examples, not runtime shell execution from JavaScript. The remaining external command risk is limited to user-invoked script execution documented by the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography lines refer to parser names, agent path checks, or frontmatter text. No hashing, encryption, signing, or credential protection code appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary text such as Claude or description fields rather than cryptographic APIs. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Benign Parsing Logic
low_findings.description: The cited lines are comments, JSON parsing catch blocks, or guidance about analyzing troubleshooting patterns. They do not collect host inventory, environment variables, network details, or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context shows local parser error handling and workflow guidance. No evidence found of host reconnaissance beyond locating known agent session folders.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Agent Directory Reads
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads from hidden home-directory agent session paths for Claude, Pi, and Codex. This is functional but sensitive because those files can contain private transcripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden directory paths are explicitly constructed or documented. The risk depends on user handling of the extracted transcript.

dangerous_patterns.title: Transcript Redirection to Temporary File
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation recommends redirecting extracted transcripts to a temporary file. This can leave sensitive session content on disk outside the original agent storage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples explicitly write transcript output to /tmp. This is common workflow behavior, but it increases exposure if the file is not removed.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need evidence from real agent sessions to improve unclear instructions. This skill extracts recent transcripts and turns usage history into focused prompts for improving or creating skills.
actual_capabilities: Finds recent Claude Code, Pi, or Codex session files for the current project.
Parses JSONL session records into a readable transcript format.
Supports explicit agent selection, working directory selection, and direct session file paths.
Generates workflows for improving an existing skill from session evidence.
Generates workflows for drafting a new skill from a successful agent session.
limitations: It reads local agent session files and can expose sensitive transcript content.
It does not redact secrets, private code, or personal data from output.
It only supports the documented Claude Code, Pi, and Codex session formats.
It creates prompts for another session rather than editing skills automatically.

use_cases.title: Improve a Reused Skill
use_cases.description: Review a session where an existing skill was confusing, then produce a prompt that asks an agent to update the skill.

use_cases.title: Capture a New Workflow
use_cases.description: Extract a productive coding session and convert the repeated commands, decisions, and pitfalls into a new skill outline.

use_cases.title: Audit Agent Friction
use_cases.description: Compare transcript evidence against current skill instructions to find missing examples, unclear steps, and repeated retries.

prompt_templates.title: Extract My Current Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use improve-skill to extract the most recent session for this project. Show me the transcript location and remind me what sensitive data to review before sharing it.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use improve-skill to analyze my latest session with the current skill. Identify missing guidance, unclear steps, and examples that should be added.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Skill from a Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use improve-skill to turn this successful agent session into a reusable skill. Focus on the workflow, commands, pitfalls, and decision rules.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Transcript Against Skill Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use improve-skill to compare the transcript with the existing skill. Produce prioritized edits, risk notes, and a concise replacement structure.

output_examples.input: A maintainer asks why a skill caused repeated retries during a coding session.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a readable transcript summary, identifies missing setup steps, and drafts an improvement prompt for a clean follow-up session.

output_examples.input: A developer wants to preserve a useful one-off debugging workflow.
output_examples.output: The skill extracts the relevant session, highlights commands and decision points, and proposes a new skill structure.

output_examples.input: A reviewer wants to share a transcript with another agent.
output_examples.output: The skill creates the transcript and warns the user to review it for secrets, private code, and personal data first.

best_practices: Review extracted transcripts for secrets, private code, and personal data before sharing them.
Use a fresh agent session for skill improvement so the transcript and current skill stay focused.
Keep final skill edits concise and based on repeated evidence from the transcript.
anti_patterns: Do not paste raw transcripts into public tools without redaction.
Do not treat a single failed command as a reason to add broad skill instructions.
Do not overwrite an existing skill before reviewing the proposed changes.

faq.question: What agent session formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Claude Code, Pi, and Codex JSONL session files in the documented local paths.

faq.question: Does it automatically edit my skills?
faq.answer: No. It helps extract transcripts and build prompts for a separate improvement session.

faq.question: Can transcript output contain secrets?
faq.answer: Yes. Session logs can contain credentials, private code, paths, and user instructions. Review output before sharing.

faq.question: Can I choose a specific session file?
faq.answer: Yes. The script accepts a direct session file path when automatic discovery is not enough.

faq.question: Why use a fresh session for improvements?
faq.answer: A fresh session keeps the task focused on the transcript and the skill being improved.

faq.question: Is this useful for marketplace skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help authors improve instructions, examples, and limitations using real usage evidence.
